articles.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
articles.summary: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

articles.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
articles.summary: The Rastriya Swatatantra Party has won a large majority in an election dominated by issues including corruption.

articles.title: France's ghost car scandal that allowed one million illegal vehicles onto the roads
articles.summary: Fake dealerships were manipulating the state vehicle licensing agency's official records, France's auditor has found.

articles.summary: The law states that children should be taught Mandarin before kindergarten, until the end of high school.

articles.title: Parents accused of hiding daughter's HIV infection and neglect
articles.summary: The child did not see a doctor until she was six - and had not had any vaccinations, Italian media say.

articles.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
articles.summary: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

articles.summary: Gunmen opened fire at Crocus City Concert hall near Moscow in 2024, killing 149 and injuring more than 500.

articles.title: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
articles.summary: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.
